contents.value:
```cpp
template<class T, class Allocator = std::allocator<T>>
class vector;
```
Dynamic contiguous array. Automatic memory management with amortized O(1) `push_back`. O(1) random access. Most commonly used container in C++.

**Header:** `<vector>`

```cpp
template<class Key, class T, class Compare = std::less<Key>,
         class Allocator = std::allocator<std::pair<const Key, T>>>
class map;
```
Sorted associative container storing unique key-value pairs using a red-black tree. O(log n) lookup, insertion, and removal. Keys are ordered by `Compare`.

**Header:** `<map>`

```cpp
template<class Key, class T, class Hash = std::hash<Key>,
         class KeyEqual = std::equal_to<Key>,
         class Allocator = std::allocator<std::pair<const Key, T>>>
class unordered_map;
```
Hash-based associative container with unique keys. Average O(1) lookup, insertion, and removal. Unordered iteration.

**Header:** `<unordered_map>`

```cpp
template<class Key, class Compare = std::less<Key>,
         class Allocator = std::allocator<Key>>
class set;
```
Sorted container of unique elements using a red-black tree. O(log n) insertion, removal, and lookup.

**Header:** `<set>`

```cpp
template<class Key, class Hash = std::hash<Key>,
         class KeyEqual = std::equal_to<Key>,
         class Allocator = std::allocator<Key>>
class unordered_set;
```
Hash-based container of unique elements. Average O(1) insertion, removal, and lookup.

**Header:** `<unordered_set>`

```cpp
using string = std::basic_string<char>;
```
Dynamic character string with automatic memory management. Supports concatenation, comparison, searching, and SSO (Small String Optimization).

**Header:** `<string>`

```cpp
using string_view = std::basic_string_view<char>;
```
Lightweight non-owning read-only reference to a character sequence. Zero-copy. Use instead of `const std::string&` for function parameters. **(C++17)**

**Header:** `<string_view>`

```cpp
template<class T, std::size_t N>
struct array;
```
Fixed-size aggregate array with STL interface. Stack-allocated with zero overhead over C-style arrays. Supports `at()` for bounds checking.

**Header:** `<array>`

```cpp
template<class T, class Allocator = std::allocator<T>>
class deque;
```
Double-ended queue with O(1) insertion and removal at both front and back. Does not guarantee contiguous storage.

**Header:** `<deque>`

```cpp
template<class T, class Allocator = std::allocator<T>>
class list;
```
Doubly-linked list with O(1) insertion and removal at any position (given an iterator). No random access.

**Header:** `<list>`

```cpp
template<class T, class Container = std::deque<T>>
class stack;
```
Container adapter providing LIFO (last-in, first-out) stack operations: `push`, `pop`, `top`.

**Header:** `<stack>`

```cpp
template<class T, class Container = std::deque<T>>
class queue;
```
Container adapter providing FIFO (first-in, first-out) queue operations: `push`, `pop`, `front`, `back`.

**Header:** `<queue>`

```cpp
template<class T, class Container = std::vector<T>,
         class Compare = std::less<typename Container::value_type>>
class priority_queue;
```
Container adapter providing constant-time access to the largest (by default) element. Implemented as a max-heap.

**Header:** `<queue>`

```cpp
template<class T1, class T2>
struct pair;
```
Holds two heterogeneous objects. Access via `.first` and `.second` or structured bindings `auto [a, b] = p;`.

**Header:** `<utility>`

```cpp
template<class... Types>
class tuple;
```
Fixed-size heterogeneous collection. Access via `std::get<N>(t)` or structured bindings. Generalizes `std::pair`.

**Header:** `<tuple>`

```cpp
template<class T>
class optional;
```
Manages an optional contained value — may or may not hold a value. Use `.value()`, `.value_or(default)`, or `*opt` to access. **(C++17)**

**Header:** `<optional>`

```cpp
template<class... Types>
class variant;
```
Type-safe discriminated union. Holds exactly one of the alternative types at any time. Access via `std::get<T>(v)` or `std::visit`. **(C++17)**

**Header:** `<variant>`

```cpp
class any;
```
Type-safe container for single values of any copy-constructible type. Access via `std::any_cast<T>(a)`. **(C++17)**

**Header:** `<any>`

```cpp
template<class T, class Deleter = std::default_delete<T>>
class unique_ptr;
```
Smart pointer with exclusive ownership semantics. Non-copyable, movable. Automatically deletes the managed object when it goes out of scope. Use `std::make_unique<T>()` to create.

**Header:** `<memory>`

```cpp
template<class T>
class shared_ptr;
```
Smart pointer with shared (reference-counted) ownership. Object is destroyed when the last `shared_ptr` owning it is destroyed. Use `std::make_shared<T>()` to create.

**Header:** `<memory>`

```cpp
template<class T>
class weak_ptr;
```
Non-owning smart pointer that references a `shared_ptr`-managed object without affecting the reference count. Use `.lock()` to obtain a `shared_ptr`. Breaks circular references.

**Header:** `<memory>`

```cpp
template<class R, class... Args>
class function<R(Args...)>;
```
General-purpose polymorphic function wrapper. Stores any callable target: functions, lambdas, bind expressions, member function pointers. Type-erased.

**Header:** `<functional>`

```cpp
template<class T, std::size_t Extent = std::dynamic_extent>
class span;
```
Non-owning view over a contiguous sequence of objects. Replaces `(T* ptr, size_t len)` pairs. Works with arrays, vectors, and C-arrays. **(C++20)**

**Header:** `<span>`

```cpp
template<class RandomIt>
void sort(RandomIt first, RandomIt last);

template<class RandomIt, class Compare>
void sort(RandomIt first, RandomIt last, Compare comp);
```
Sorts elements in [first, last) in ascending order using introsort. O(n log n) guaranteed.

**Header:** `<algorithm>`

```cpp
template<class InputIt, class T>
InputIt find(InputIt first, InputIt last, const T& value);
```
Returns an iterator to the first element equal to `value` in [first, last). Returns `last` if not found. O(n).

**Header:** `<algorithm>`

```cpp
template<class InputIt, class OutputIt, class UnaryOp>
OutputIt transform(InputIt first, InputIt last,
                   OutputIt d_first, UnaryOp op);
```
Applies `op` to each element and writes the result to `d_first`. Functional-style map operation.

**Header:** `<algorithm>`

```cpp
template<class InputIt, class T>
T accumulate(InputIt first, InputIt last, T init);

template<class InputIt, class T, class BinaryOp>
T accumulate(InputIt first, InputIt last, T init, BinaryOp op);
```
Computes a left fold over [first, last) starting with `init`. Default operation is addition.

**Header:** `<numeric>`

```cpp
template<class T>
constexpr std::remove_reference_t<T>&& move(T&& t) noexcept;
```
Casts to an rvalue reference, indicating the argument may be "moved from." Enables efficient resource transfer via move constructors/assignment operators.

**Header:** `<utility>`

```cpp
template<class T>
constexpr T&& forward(std::remove_reference_t<T>& t) noexcept;
```
Forwards lvalues as lvalues and rvalues as rvalues. Essential for perfect forwarding in template code.

**Header:** `<utility>`

```cpp
template<class T, class... Args>
unique_ptr<T> make_unique(Args&&... args); // (C++14)
```
Creates a `std::unique_ptr` managing a new object constructed with the given arguments. Exception-safe. Preferred over raw `new`.

**Header:** `<memory>`

```cpp
template<class T, class... Args>
shared_ptr<T> make_shared(Args&&... args);
```
Creates a `std::shared_ptr` with a single allocation for both the object and the control block. More efficient than `shared_ptr<T>(new T(...))`.

**Header:** `<memory>`

```cpp
extern std::ostream cout;
```
The standard output stream associated with the C stream `stdout`. Thread-safe for individual operations when synchronized with C I/O (default).

**Header:** `<iostream>`

```cpp
extern std::istream cin;
```
The standard input stream associated with the C stream `stdin`. Tied to `std::cout` by default for prompt flushing.

**Header:** `<iostream>`

```cpp
template<class CharT, class Traits>
std::basic_ostream<CharT, Traits>&
  endl(std::basic_ostream<CharT, Traits>& os);
```
Inserts `'\n'` and flushes the stream. Use `'\n'` instead when flushing is not needed for better performance.

**Header:** `<ostream>`

```cpp
template<class... Args>
std::string format(std::format_string<Args...> fmt, Args&&... args);
```
Type-safe Python-style string formatting. Supports positional args, alignment, fill, precision, and type specifiers. **(C++20)**

**Header:** `<format>`

```cpp
namespace std::ranges { ... }
```
The Ranges library providing composable, lazy range adaptors and constrained algorithms. Includes `views::filter`, `views::transform`, `views::take`, `views::drop`, `views::reverse`, and more. **(C++20)**

**Header:** `<ranges>`

```cpp
auto x = 42;           // int
auto s = "hello"s;     // std::string
auto [k, v] = pair;    // structured bindings (C++17)
auto fn = [](auto x) { return x; }; // generic lambda (C++14)
```
Automatic type deduction from the initializer expression. Also used for function return type deduction and generic lambdas.

```cpp
constexpr std::nullptr_t nullptr;
```
The null pointer literal of type `std::nullptr_t`. Preferred over `NULL` or `0` for pointer initialization. Unambiguous in overload resolution.

```cpp
class thread;
```
Represents a single thread of execution. Construct with a callable. Must be `join()`ed or `detach()`ed before destruction.

**Header:** `<thread>`

```cpp
class mutex;
```
Provides basic mutual exclusion. Non-recursive. Use `std::lock_guard` or `std::unique_lock` for RAII-style locking.

**Header:** `<mutex>`

```cpp
template<class InputIt, class UnaryFunc> UnaryFunc for_each(InputIt first, InputIt last, UnaryFunc f)
```
Applies the given function to each element in [first, last). Returns the function object after all applications.

```cpp
template<class T> void swap(T& a, T& b) noexcept(...)
```
Exchanges the values of a and b. Uses move semantics when available.
